author: Waryam Singh, Bhai
title: Nawan Qissa Ghund wali Nar
gloss: Love verses. The story ends in the marriage of the lover and his beloved.
publisher: The author, Dhádari Kalán, district Ludhiána

author: Barkat Ali
title: Si Harfii Sadaf-i-Aksir
notes: Serial 37 continues Punjabi—Poetry across the Persian-character subsection.

title: Baran Mah Wajog
gloss: Love verses in the form of a calendar.
notes: Bracket note: previous edition noticed at entry No. 115 on page 13 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March 1898.

author: Dewa Ram, Lala
title: Qissa Raja Mur Dhaj
gloss: The story of a Raja who was a great devotee of Krishna. His son seized the horse set at large by the Pandavas and when Arjana complained to Krishna, the latter remarked that he would not permit war to be waged against his devotee. Krishna then rode a lion and took Arjana with him to test Mor Dhaj who slaughtered his son to feed the lion.
notes: Raja Mordhwaj bhakti legend, 5,000 copies; Hindu author, Muslim booksellers — the Krishna-devotee king who feeds his son to a lion for a guest.

author: Fazl-i-Karim
title: Si Harfi Raja Hayat Khan
gloss: Verses describing the good qualities of Raja Hayat Khan of Usman, district Jhelum.

author: Gauri Shankar
title: Man dian Mattiyan Sharabi Puttar nun
gloss: Admonitory verses by a mother to her son who was addicted to wine-drinking.
publisher: The author, Honorary Secretary, Temperance Society, Majitha, Amritsar district
notes: Temperance verse ('A mother's counsel to her drunkard son'), free — the temperance movement already active in Q1 (cf. the Amritsar Temperance Society's coordinated campaign in Q4).
